world.title: CCC members jailed 22 years for Mbare political murder
world.description: The High Court has sentenced three Citizens Coalition for Change (CCC) members to 22 years and nine months in prison each for the murder of a man during a violent political clash with ZANU PF supporters in Harare. Justice Esther Muremba condemned the killing of Onisimo Mavhungire as a brutal mob attack carried out in […]

world.title: Imam fined RM8,000 for recording video of cousin showering
world.description: The part-time imam at a Shah Alam surau pleads guilty to outraging the 27-year-old victim's modesty at a house in Ketereh, Kelantan.

world.description: In the fourth quarter of 2025, house prices in the EU rose by 5.5%, while rents increased by 3.2% compared with the fourth quarter of 2024, according to Eurostat data published on April 7th.

business.title: India orders major airports to cut some charges by 25%, in relief to airlines
business.description: The move to cut charges comes after IndiGo and Air India sought rationalisation of some fees levied by airports. Both airlines are facing a double whammy from the Iran war, as Indian airlines are already barred from flying over Pakistan.

science.title: Quantum computers keep losing data. This breakthrough finally tracks it
science.description: Quantum computers struggle with a major flaw: their information vanishes unpredictably. Scientists have now created a new method that can measure this loss over 100 times faster than before. By tracking changes in near real time, researchers can finally see what’s going wrong inside these systems. This could be a big step toward making quantum computers stable and practical.

science.title: See first images from Artemis II's Moon fly-by released NASA
science.description: NASA has released the first photographs taken by the Artemis II astronauts during their fly-by of the Moon.

science.title: NASA releases picture of 'Earthset' shot by Artemis II crew
science.description: NASA on Tuesday released a photograph of Earth dipping below the lunar horizon, more than 57 years after an iconic "Earthrise" image was captured by an Apollo 8 astronaut.

science.title: Climate change may speed evolution through inherited gene regulation changes
science.description: A new paper in Molecular Biology and Evolution, finds that changes in animal development induced by climate shock persist generations after the initial event. The escalating effects of climate change are likely to, in effect, speed up evolution.

ai.title: Google AI Overviews May Give Thousands Of Incorrect Answers Daily
ai.description: A new study cited by The New York Times claims Google’s Gemini-powered AI Overviews answers correctly about 90% of the time, meaning roughly 1 in 10 responses could be wrong. On Google’s massive scale, that could translate into thousands of inaccurate AI-generated answers daily., Technology & Science, Times Now

ai.title: Art forgers and the secrets of authenticity
ai.description: Fraudsters use sophisticated techniques such as style mimicry, antique materials and AI tools. The victims are mostly buyers who are lured by low prices without adequate checks. New law introduces a stricter framework, increases penalties and strengthens prevention

ai.title: Sam Altman resets Codex after Anthropic announces terrifying Claude Mythos update
ai.description: Anthropic recently announced Claude Mythos, an AI so powerful that it can hack almost any major browser or operating system. Meanwhile, Anthropic’s rival, OpenAI, celebrated getting 3 million users for its own coding agent Codex, by resetting usage limits for all users. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced the limit reset on X.

ai.title: Anthropic ropes in Microsoft executive Eric Boyd to lead infrastructure
ai.description: Anthropic has hired former Microsoft executive Eric Boyd to lead its infrastructure team as demand for its AI tools grows. The company’s revenue has surged, driven by products like Claude Code. However, rising usage has strained services, prompting major investments in computing capacity and data centres to support future expansion.
